mnemonics: SRS CRACKS: Stands for Software Requirements Specification Characteristics - Complete, Realizable, All-encompassing, Consistent, KISS (Keep It Simple), Secure
keyPoints: Software engineering applies systematic, disciplined, quantifiable approaches to software development
The software development lifecycle (SDLC) includes requirements gathering, design, implementation, testing, deployment, and maintenance
Agile methodologies emphasize iterative development, customer collaboration, and responding to change
commonMistakes: Skipping proper requirements analysis leads to building the wrong product
Underestimating testing time and effort results in buggy releases
Poor communication with stakeholders causes misalignment between expectations and deliverables
